chapters.title: What is pre-suasion?
chapters.text:
Throughout its existence, humanity has created many pre-suasion techniques. Only some are easy to implement, but a method can be simple to produce good results. There are a few basic pre-suasion options that will be effective despite their simplicity.

• Reciprocation. People give back in return if they feel they owe you. A gift you offer should be meaningful and unexpected. What is more important is that it has to be customized. The restaurants that provide free snacks before ordering get better tips in return.
• Liking. To get attention, salespeople get customers to like them. They make jokes, look nice, and smile more. They also emphasize similarities and provide compliments. People want to share language styles, gestures, nationality, or even a favorite sports team. Compliments also work!
• Social proof. The audience compares their responses to others to get social proof. A more popular response sounds right, and people tend to follow those who think the same way. For instance, to increase the demand for a particular dish, restaurant owners must label it “the most popular choice.” The social-proof messages are very effective when they are valid and feasible.
• Authority. People are more likely to believe in a message from an authoritative communicator recognized for their expertise and trustworthiness. It also works well when you use transition words, like however, but, or yet. They take attention away from weakness.
• Scarcity. Shortage drives desire because people are afraid to lose a chance to get it. It also increases the judged value of that particular item. Limited edition collections of cosmetics or cars are always in great demand because it is enjoyable to feel like the owner of something special. Those approaches may double sales.
• Consistency. Consistency helps to persuade people to be more committed if they start with tiny actions. If a speaker persuades us to do something small, the desire for consistency prompts a person to make the next more significant step.

You can't be afraid of communication starting if you take the initiative.

To make a perfect strategy, build a positive association using reciprocation and liking. Then, minimize uncertainty and increase confidence with social proof and authority. Finally, motivate with consistency and scarcity.

chapters.title: Channeling attention, connecting hearts
chapters.text:
It's a natural tendency to hunt for confirmations, and, as the success hunter, you should use it. Even scientists have confirmed this trend many times through various experiments. For example, in one of them, people were asked to help conduct a survey, but less than a third agreed to this request. Then, the scientists used a pre-suasive opener, questioning, “Are you a helpful person?” before asking for assistance. The result was predictable: 77% volunteered.

This little thick can teach you a lot. If you want to persuade, try transforming others' beliefs, attitudes, or experiences to fit the behavior. The best strategy is the nontraditional communication model based on channeled attention. You can start a conversation with an opener to change what is essential for a person at the moment of decision. Give the other's brain a hint; they will be likelier to live up to your expectations.

This technique can even affect self-esteem. In a similar survey, the scientist asked people if they consider themselves adventurous before offering them to provide an email address and get information about a free sample of a new soft drink. Almost 76% said yes and gave their emails. Nearly magical, but at the same time, it's dangerous.

Relationship is not a short-distance race but a long marathon.

Any technique should be used ethically. Here, people were ready to share private information with strangers. Even if your interlocutor is prepared to do something like this, they may feel vulnerable later. Using the information to harm them could harm your relationship in the long run. Pay attention to this.

Repeat it. “Pay attention.” It's an excellent phrase that summarizes everything said. The human brain can concentrate only on one thing at a time. If you say you are multitasking, you can quickly switch attention from one thing to another, experiencing attentional blinks in between or a dead mental spot. That's why you pay the price when you lose focus. However, you can also turn this disadvantage into a benefit. Other's brain works the same way! Channel attention using the described method to become extra persuasive.

chapters.title: Stimulate, not simulate
chapters.text:
Every interaction is filled with a lot of stimuli. Like invisible mystical creatures, they whisper what to do and how to do it in your ear. They live in everyone, growing into the human essence since ancient times. Do you want not to simulate interest but stimulate it? Use them!

Learn the other person's preferences to impress them.

Let's start with a simple idea. If you want to know what kind of stimulus to use for an opener to make a conversation effective, think about what is relevant to your audience. People pay attention to a stimulus that fits better to their goal in a particular situation. Here are some of the most common types.

• The sexual. A prominent sexual boost can direct human attention from other things. But an opener has to be in place to make it happen. Advertisements often depict sexuality to sell cosmetics, perfumes, and clothes, but not furniture or kitchen appliances. Sexuality works on someone who has already subconsciously associated something with it. Are you going to a beach party? Feel free to use this stimulus in conversation. Are you at a business meeting with the CEO? Avoid making such moves.
• The threatening. Violence and danger influence decisions. For example, people switch from airplanes to cars after terrorist attacks, though the chance of dying in a car accident is much higher. The large images on cigarette packages scare off smokers and nonsmokers. If you don't want to seem dangerous, use the opposite tactic: a chilling message in the middle of the life storm will be appreciated.
• You. The information about oneself is a mighty magnet of attention. Individualized messages attract so severely that even using the personal pronoun “you” instead of “they” during public speech usually has an enormous effect. Total control of other's reactions is unreal and cruel, but at least you can receive it.
• The unfinished. People remember unfinished tasks better than completed ones. Intrigue makes the mind return to the unfinished statement again and again. You can even fight your procrastination by leaving some part of your work unfinished so that you have the urge to return to it.

These incentives can be incredibly powerful, whether you use them or identify them to cut off someone else's influence.

chapters.title: The power of associations
chapters.text:
Association is a powerful tool that has evolved with human civilization over the centuries. They were even necessary for survival, allowing our ancestors to draw a connection between the forest, darkness, and the probable danger of being eaten by a tiger. So, this ancient mechanism is deeply ingrained in the minds of every person, and it can and should be used.

Language is the most effective way to associate because mental activity connects exact words with images and pleasant and unpleasant feelings. Therefore, the correct phrases can have an incredible emotional effect and efficiency.

Scientific researches show that hostile language increases subsequent aggressiveness. On the other side, motivational language with words like “to win,” “to succeed,” and “to achieve” increases performance and more than doubles the desire to proceed to work on something.

Do you want your audience to listen to your every word? Speak to them as a good friend, not a fierce critic.

There is no question that information about the self is an exceedingly powerful magnet of attention. ~ Robert Cialdini

One of the most powerful tools of influence is metaphor. It forces a person to create a stable association with a vivid image that quickly sticks in the memory. For example, a politician using a well-introduced metaphor helps voters believe one's approach to a problem is correct. A metaphor works even nonverbally. On a cold day, hold a cup of hot coffee in your hands, and you will automatically feel cozy even when surrounded by strangers.

One of the best examples is the case of a prominent salesman from Ohio, Ben Feldman. He used the metaphor “walked out” of life to talk about death. “When you walk out,” he said, “your insurance money walks in.” The success was almost enormous; in the 1970s-1980s, he sold more insurance than hundreds of agencies around the US.

A personalized approach to a person wins their sympathy better than a general one.

Some of the advertisement stimuli focus on uniqueness and personalization. 2013 Coca-Cola replaced its name on bottles with 150 most common first names. Sales have skyrocketed! It demonstrates that the self-centered concept works well in Western culture. If you are working with Eastern countries, group-linked words will work better.

chapters.title: The best pre-suasive practices
chapters.text:
Throughout its existence, humanity has created many pre-suasion techniques. Only some are easy to implement, but a method can be simple to produce good results. There are a few basic pre-suasion options that will be effective despite their simplicity.

• Reciprocation. People give back in return if they feel they owe you. A gift you offer should be meaningful and unexpected. What is more important is that it has to be customized. The restaurants that provide free snacks before ordering get better tips in return.

• Authority. People are more likely to believe in a message from an authoritative communicator recognized for their expertise and trustworthiness. It also works well when you use transition words, like however, but, or yet. They take attention away from weakness.

chapters.title: Uniting and growing stronger
chapters.text:
Apart from those six practices, there is another one, the most complex and powerful. It's about relationships. Whatever the reason for unity across race, family, and nationality — it compels people to help each other. Social scientists discovered two main factors that lead to the feeling of “being at one.”

Being in unison is the first genetically proven way to unite, mostly in kinship. People are incredibly ready to help their close relatives. Concepts like brothers, sisterhood, forefathers, motherland, and heritage produce more extraordinary self-sacrifice. Coming from one place, a home, or a region contributes to it. Blood connections also encourage people to take examples from other kinship members, even those who do not belong to their community.

To teach a person something, you need not only to talk but to show an example.

A great example is the story of Japanese Chiune Sugihara. Although Japan was an ally of the Nazis, he risked his life to help foreign Jews hide during the Holocaust. His father once owned a hotel and always showed how important it is to help strangers in need.

Acting jointly produces similar results as with kinship. To use it pre-suasively, influencers increase levels of liking and provide more significant support. When people act together, their positive feelings about one another increase. It may be something simple, like synchronized smiling, but after, people feel more positive about one another. That's why group exercises, singing, or dancing are often used in various lectures and trainings to relax.

Music isn’t about such practicalities. It’s about harmonies — melodic ones that lead to emotional and relational ones. ~ Robert Cialdini

Co-creation works similarly. Drawing, embroidery, and constructing an Ikea table can be an excellent background for conversing with your loved ones.

Did you know? Marketers often use red on clearance sale signs because it can create a sense of urgency and excitement, priming shoppers to make faster purchasing decisions.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
It is possible to sway someone, persuade them to do something, and incline them to make decisions. Pre-suasion techniques, meaning using psychological patterns to influence a person's attitude, will significantly help you. You can learn them too and succeed in various situations like a magician. Just don't forget about ethics!

Try this



• Suggest a joint activity for the first meeting. Doing something together brings people closer together.
• Use positive vocabulary. Negativity subconsciously causes anxiety.
• Emphasize similarities. People like to see something in common with others.
